message:
feat(hostcall): add HostcallRewriteEngine for cost-aware marshalling path selection

Introduce a constrained hostcall rewrite planner that evaluates
alternative marshalling strategies against a baseline and selects
the lowest-cost plan when semantics are preserved:

- New module `src/hostcall_rewrite.rs` (159 lines):
  - HostcallRewritePlanKind enum: BaselineCanonical vs FastOpcodeFusion
  - HostcallRewritePlan: carries kind, estimated_cost, and rule_id
  - HostcallRewriteDecision: selected plan + cost delta + fallback reason
  - HostcallRewriteEngine: env-gated (PI_HOSTCALL_EGRAPH_REWRITE)
    with select_plan() that picks the cheapest candidate whose cost
    is strictly less than baseline, falling back with reason tracking
  - Unit tests covering: baseline-only, single better candidate,
    multiple candidates, equal-cost tie-breaking, env disable

- Integrated into `src/extensions.rs` marshalling hot-path:
  - HostcallPayloadArena::marshal() now computes both baseline and
    fast-opcode hashes, verifies hash equivalence (semantic safety),
    then delegates to HostcallRewriteEngine::select_plan() to choose
    the optimal path rather than short-circuiting on first fast hit
  - New constants: REWRITE_COST_BASELINE (100), REWRITE_COST_FAST_OPCODE (35),
    rule IDs, and divergence fallback reason
  - HostcallMarshallingTelemetry extended with rewrite_rule,
    expected/observed cost deltas, and rewrite_fallback_reason
  - New atomic REWRITE_FALLBACK_COUNTER for divergence monitoring
  - Global OnceLock-backed hostcall_rewrite_engine() singleton

- `src/lib.rs`: declare `pub mod hostcall_rewrite` with alphabetical
  ordering fix (flake_classifier now sorts after extensions_js)

The rewrite engine is enabled by default and can be disabled by
setting PI_HOSTCALL_EGRAPH_REWRITE=0|false|off|disabled. When
disabled, all marshalling falls through to baseline canonical path
with zero overhead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
